_description: Privacy and consent framework for dome data domains. Maps each dome layer and data source type to applicable privacy laws, consent requirements, and de-identification rules. The assembler checks this before admitting new fragment sources. Currently all fragments are public data (Census, BLS, HUD, EPA) — no consent needed. This becomes critical when HMIS, behavioral health, or justice data enters the pipeline.

_sources.hipaa: 45 CFR Parts 160, 164 — Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
_sources.42cfr: 42 CFR Part 2 — Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder Records
_sources.ferpa: 20 USC § 1232g — Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
_sources.vawa: 34 USC § 12291 — Violence Against Women Act (HMIS protections)
_sources.hhid: HUD HMIS Data Standards — Client-level data protection

public.description: Aggregate statistics, no individual records, freely available

protected_health.de_identification: Safe Harbor (remove 18 identifiers) or Expert Determination

substance_use.description: Substance use disorder diagnosis, treatment, or referral records
substance_use.consent_type: Dual written consent — more restrictive than HIPAA

education_records.description: Student education records maintained by educational institutions
education_records.consent_type: Written consent from parent (under 18) or student

hmis_client.description: Client-level data in Homeless Management Information System
hmis_client.consent_type: Informed consent at intake; VAWA survivors can opt out entirely

justice_records.description: Criminal justice records, arrest records, incarceration data
justice_records.governing_law: Varies by state — CORI, state privacy acts
justice_records.consent_type: Written authorization; some records sealed/expunged by law
justice_records.ban_the_box: Many jurisdictions restrict employer/housing access to records

financial_records.description: Credit reports, bank records, tax returns

hud-pit-homeless.notes: Aggregate PIT counts, not client data

cdc-places-*.notes: Model-based county estimates, no individual data

FUTURE:hmis-client.notes: DO NOT INGEST without data sharing agreement and client consent

FUTURE:behavioral-health.notes: 42 CFR Part 2 — dual written consent BEFORE any fragment creation

FUTURE:hospital-discharge.notes: HIPAA authorization or BAA with covered entity

FUTURE:school-records.notes: FERPA consent from parent or eligible student

FUTURE:criminal-history.notes: State CORI laws apply; many records sealed

de_identification_rules.suppression_note: Census already suppresses cells < 11; BATHS fragments inherit this protection
